summary:
Lamina-associated domains (LADs) are primarily associated with transcriptionally inactive or silenced genes due to their localization at the nuclear periphery. There is no supporting evidence from available literature linking them directly to transcriptional activation; instead, studies indicate such domains correlate more strongly with repression than activation. 

Final Answer: B
